pattern: A palindrome pairs characters symmetrically, so the answer is twice the number of full pairs of each letter plus one optional center if any letter has an odd count.
hints: A palindrome reads the same both ways, so every character except possibly a single middle one must appear an even number of times.
Count how many of each letter you have; each pair of identical letters can sit symmetrically around the centre.
Sum the largest even number that is at most each letter's count, and if any letter had an odd count add one more for the centre.

edge_inputs.description: mixed letters with one odd count
case sensitivity means upper and lower differ
all distinct letters keeps one as centre
all identical odd count uses all letters
two odd counts contribute one centre only

stress.description: random mixed-case letters so many odd and even counts appear
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    alphabet = string.ascii_letters
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))]
stress.note: full mixed-case alphabet keeps case sensitivity under test
